message:
Add preset system and expand main functionality

New features:
- Preset design documentation
- Expanded main.rs with preset support
- Better configuration options

Documentation:
- docs/preset-design.md: Preset system design

Beads tracking updated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
 {"id":"bd-152","title":"Implement session diff logic","description":"Deliverables:
- DiffResult struct:
  - new_domains: Vec<String>
  - removed_domains: Vec<String>
  - changed_domains: Vec<(String, i64, i64)> (domain, old_count, new_count)
  - new_processes: Vec<String>
  - provider_changes: HashMap<Provider, (i64, i64)>

- compute_session_diff(old_run_id: &str, new_run_id: &str, threshold_pct: f64) -> DiffResult:
  - Query domain counts for each session
  - Compute set differences
  - Filter changes by threshold

- CLI: rano diff --old <id> --new <id> [--threshold <pct>] [--json]
- Pretty output: sections for each diff type
- JSON output: DiffResult as JSON

- Unit tests for diff computation
- E2E test with seeded sessions","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:12:13.251408999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:12:13.269662785Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-152","depends_on_id":"bd-1o5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:12:13.269634692Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-15l","title":"Add ancestry CLI flag and SQLite storage","description":"Deliverables:
- CLI flag: --show-ancestry (default: false)
- Config key: show_ancestry=true

- Event output with ancestry:
  Pretty: '2026-01-20T10:00:00Z | + connect | anthropic | zsh → tmux → claude (pid=1234) | ...'
  JSON: add 'ancestry': ['zsh(100)', 'tmux(200)', 'claude(1234)']

- SQLite schema update:
  - Add 'ancestry_path' TEXT column to events table
  - Store as comma-separated: 'zsh:100,tmux:200,claude:1234'
  - Index on ancestry_path for queries

- Report enhancement:
  - Group by top-level parent (first in chain)
  - Show: 'Spawned from zsh: 45 connections, tmux: 23 connections'","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:03:20.237572582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:04:17.272335845Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15l","depends_on_id":"bd-1ju","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:03:20.308039948Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-15l","depends_on_id":"bd-vb7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:04:17.270912144Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

  - Show: 'Spawned from zsh: 45 connections, tmux: 23 connections'","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:03:20.237572582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:07:53.478986533Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:07:53.478919266Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-15l","depends_on_id":"bd-1ju","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:03:20.308039948Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-15l","depends_on_id":"bd-vb7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:04:17.270912144Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"bd-15l","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented --show-ancestry flag + config key (show_ancestry). Report now includes Spawned From section (top-level parent counts) and JSON ancestry_roots. Note: cargo test -q attempted but was blocked by concurrent cargo lock in shared env.","created_at":"2026-01-21T10:07:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1gl","title":"Implement export subcommand","description":"Deliverables:
- Add 'export' to Command enum
- ExportArgs struct with all filter fields
- parse_export_args() function

- run_export() function:
  1. Open SQLite (error if missing)
  2. Build query with WHERE clause from filters
  3. Stream rows to stdout in requested format
  4. Handle Ctrl+C gracefully (don't corrupt output)

- format_csv_row(row) function:
  - Escape quotes by doubling
  - Wrap fields with commas/quotes/newlines in quotes
  - Join with comma, output CRLF

- format_jsonl_row(row) function:
  - Serialize row to JSON object
  - Output with LF

- Memory efficiency:
  - Use SQLite row iteration, not collect()
  - Flush stdout periodically

- Error handling:
  - Missing SQLite file: clear error message
  - Empty result set: output header only (CSV) or nothing (JSONL)
  - Invalid filter: exit with error before query","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:42.648931351Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T08:59:34.850375758Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T08:59:34.850143029Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gl","depends_on_id":"bd-1vz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:42.684768291Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gl","depends_on_id":"bd-2a6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:57.715021911Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"bd-1gl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented export subcommand in src/main.rs (CLI parsing, query builder, CSV/JSONL formatting, streaming + flush, Ctrl+C handling). Added unit tests for csv_escape/glob_to_sql_like/validate_fields. cargo test -q passed.","created_at":"2026-01-21T08:59:15Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1i6","title":"Connection retry detection","description":"Purpose:
- Detect and highlight reconnection patterns (same remote, repeated connect/close cycles)
- Surface rate limiting, network issues, or bugs in AI clients

User impact:
- See warning: '⚠ Retry pattern: 5 connections to api.anthropic.com:443 in 30s'
- Identify problematic connection behavior without manual analysis
- Correlate retries with provider responses

Features:
- Track connection events per (remote_ip, remote_port, pid)
- Configurable threshold: --retry-threshold <N> (default 3)
- Configurable window: --retry-window-ms <N> (default 60000)
- Output retry warnings inline with events
- Store retry_count in SQLite for analysis

Implementation:
- HashMap<(IpAddr, u16, u32), Vec<Instant>> for recent connections
- On close event: check if count in window exceeds threshold
- Prune old entries on check (sliding window)

Testing:
- Unit tests for retry detection logic
- E2E script that simulates rapid reconnects
- Verify warnings appear and SQLite has retry counts","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:56.071844384Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:56.101211940Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1i6","depends_on_id":"bd-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:56.101171213Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jj","title":"Design alert system architecture","description":"Deliverables:
- AlertConfig struct with all threshold fields
- Decision on pattern matching library (regex vs glob vs both)
- Define alert event structure for SQLite storage
- Define alert output format (stderr, prefix, colors)
- Decide on alert state tracking (cooldown to avoid spam)

Design questions to answer:
1. Should alerts be per-connection or per-threshold-breach?
2. How to handle alert cooldown (same alert within N seconds)?
3. Should we track 'alert acknowledged' state?
4. How do alerts interact with --json output mode?

Output:
- Design doc in docs/alert-design.md
- Updated schema proposal for events table","design":"Design decisions (pre-answered to speed implementation):
1. Alerts should be per-connection (each matching event triggers alert, subject to cooldown)
2. Cooldown: HashMap<AlertType, Instant> - skip alert if same type within cooldown_ms
3. No 'acknowledged' state for v1 - keep it simple, user handles alerts
4. JSON mode: alerts as JSON objects on stderr with alert:true field, same structure as events

Pattern matching approach:
- Use glob for simple wildcards (most common case)
- Allow regex with r: prefix for power users (e.g., 'r:.*evil.*')
- Default to glob if no prefix","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:28.505557159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T07:38:54.195422658Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T07:38:54.195219435Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jj","depends_on_id":"bd-2iu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:28.527150866Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- CLI flags: --retry-threshold <N>, --retry-window-ms <N>
- Config keys: retry_threshold, retry_window_ms
- Warning output format: '⚠ Retry pattern: N connections to IP:port in Xs'

- Unit tests:
  - test_retry_detection_triggers_at_threshold()
  - test_retry_detection_window_expiry()
  - test_retry_detection_per_endpoint()

- E2E test: simulate rapid reconnects, verify warnings","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:10:15.710962523Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:10:49.214866797Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-90z","depends_on_id":"bd-1i6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:10:15.753292539Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-90z","depends_on_id":"bd-3dy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:10:49.214275153Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-94z","title":"Ancestry tests and documentation","description":"Deliverables:
- Unit tests:
  - test_read_ancestry_single_level() - process with no parent
  - test_read_ancestry_multi_level() - 3+ level chain
  - test_ancestry_cache_hit() - cached value returned
  - test_ancestry_cache_staleness() - recompute on comm mismatch
  - test_format_ancestry_truncation() - >5 levels truncated

- E2E test script (tests/e2e/process-ancestry.sh):
  - Create nested process tree: bash → sleep (to simulate claude)
  - Run rano --show-ancestry --pid <sleep_pid>
  - Verify ancestry displayed in output
  - Verify ancestry stored in SQLite
  - Verify report shows parent breakdown
  - Log: process tree, expected ancestry, actual output

- Documentation:
  - README section: 'Process Ancestry'
  - Use case: debugging agent swarms
  - Example output with ancestry
  - Performance note: caching behavior","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:03:51.718741595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:04:18.655383269Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-94z","depends_on_id":"bd-15l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:04:18.654742192Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-94z","depends_on_id":"bd-1ju","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:03:51.785639198Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- Unit tests:

  - test_validate_unknown_key() - warning for typo

  - test_validate_invalid_pattern() - error for bad regex
  - test_validate_missing_path() - warning for nonexistent log_dir
  - test_validate_toml_invalid_mode() - error for bad mode
  - test_config_show_output() - correct format

- Documentation:
  - README section: 'Configuration Validation'
  - Example: 'rano config check' workflow
  - Error message reference
  - Troubleshooting common issues","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:39.771915161Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:44.985444550Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b6k","depends_on_id":"bd-300","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:44.985422268Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-b6k","depends_on_id":"bd-n2d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:39.785942076Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-db8","title":"Design preset system","description":"Deliverables:
- Preset specification:
  - File format: same key=value as config.conf
  - File location: ~/.config/rano/presets/<name>.conf
  - Precedence: CLI > preset > config.conf > defaults

- Define built-in presets (embedded in binary):
  - audit.conf: for security auditing
  - quiet.conf: minimal output
  - live.conf: real-time monitoring focus
  - verbose.conf: maximum detail

- CLI design:
  - --preset <name>: load named preset
  - --list-presets: show available presets with descriptions
  - Multiple --preset flags: merge in order (later wins)

- Error handling:
  - Unknown preset name: list available and exit
  - Invalid preset file: warn and continue with partial

- Output: docs/preset-design.md with examples","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:05:35.453296237Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:05:35.568148529Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db8","depends_on_id":"bd-26d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:05:35.567500528Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-fb6","title":"Implement shell status command","description":"Deliverables:
- StatusArgs struct:
  - one_line: bool
  - format: Option<String>
  - sqlite: PathBuf

- Template expansion:
  - {active}: active connection count
  - {total}: total connections
  - {anthropic}, {openai}, {google}: per-provider counts
  - {session_name}: current session name

- run_status(args: &StatusArgs) -> Result:
  - Query latest session from SQLite
  - Expand template or use default format
  - Output single line

- Performance: target <50ms execution
- Caching: optional, skip for simplicity initially

- Unit tests for template expansion
- Benchmark test for execution time","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:12:22.021244940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:12:22.041877917Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fb6","depends_on_id":"bd-36b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:12:22.041849373Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-lr2","title":"Alert output formatting and logging","description":"Deliverables:
- Alert output to stderr with distinct formatting:
  [ALERT] 2026-01-20T10:00:00Z | domain-watch | api.unknown.com matched '*unknown*'
  [ALERT] 2026-01-20T10:00:01Z | max-connections | 101 connections (threshold: 100)
  [ALERT] 2026-01-20T10:00:02Z | long-duration | connection to api.anthropic.com:443 lasted 45000ms

- Colors:
  - Alert prefix: bright red background
  - Alert type: yellow
  - Details: white

- Bell: if --alert-bell, output \a after alert line

- JSON mode: alerts as JSON objects on stderr with 'alert': true field

- SQLite: add 'alert' boolean column to events table
  - Alerts logged with alert=true for later querying

- Summary: include alert count in final summary
  'Alerts triggered: 5 (3 domain-watch, 2 max-connections)'","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:54.170178182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T08:23:11.295641189Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lr2","depends_on_id":"bd-20u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:10.488350819Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lr2","depends_on_id":"bd-291","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:10.788894643Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lr2","depends_on_id":"bd-2iu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:54.200230439Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

  'Alerts triggered: 5 (3 domain-watch, 2 max-connections)'","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:54.170178182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:54:52.869084797Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lr2","depends_on_id":"bd-20u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:10.488350819Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lr2","depends_on_id":"bd-291","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:10.788894643Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-lr2","depends_on_id":"bd-2iu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:00:54.200230439Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-n2d","title":"Config validation command","description":"Purpose:
- Catch configuration errors before runtime
- Reduce debugging time for 'why isn't my config working'
- Provide clear feedback on config file issues

User impact:
- 'rano config check' validates all config files and reports issues
- 'rano config show' displays resolved configuration
- Clear error messages point to exact file:line of problems

Features:
- rano config check: validate config.conf, rano.toml, presets
- rano config show: display resolved config as key=value
- rano config show --json: display as JSON for automation
- rano config paths: show where config files are loaded from

Validation checks:
- Syntax: valid key=value, valid TOML
- Known keys: warn on unknown keys (typo detection)
- Value types: numbers parse, booleans valid
- Patterns: regex/glob patterns compile
- Paths: referenced files exist (log_dir, sqlite)
- Provider config: patterns are valid, mode is merge/replace

Exit codes:
- 0: valid configuration
- 1: errors found (won't run)
- 2: warnings only (will run but may have issues)

Testing:
- Unit tests for each validation check
- E2E script with invalid configs to verify error messages","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:13.564851300Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:13.594283838Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-n2d","depends_on_id":"bd-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:06:13.594253010Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-q9u","title":"Automatic session naming","description":"Purpose:
- Generate human-readable session names instead of opaque run_ids
- Enable 'remember that session last Tuesday' conversations

User impact:
- Sessions named like 'morning-claude-audit-2026-01-20' instead of '12345-1705512345000'
- 'rano report --session morning-claude-audit-2026-01-20' for lookup
- Easier to discuss sessions in team contexts

Features:
- Auto-generate name on session start: {time_of_day}-{primary_provider}-{pattern}-{date}
- --session-name <name> to override auto-generated name
- Store in sessions table alongside run_id
- Report and export accept session name as filter

Time of day mapping:
- 00:00-06:00: night
- 06:00-12:00: morning
- 12:00-18:00: afternoon
- 18:00-24:00: evening

Testing:
- Unit tests for name generation
- E2E script verifying names appear in report
- Test custom --session-name override","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:05.697380819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:05.727882240Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-q9u","depends_on_id":"bd-3o1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:07:05.727782292Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-uj6","title":"Export tests and documentation","description":"Deliverables:
- Unit tests:
  - test_csv_escape_quotes() - 'foo"bar' -> '"foo""bar"'
  - test_csv_escape_comma() - 'foo,bar' wrapped in quotes
  - test_csv_escape_newline() - embedded newlines wrapped
  - test_csv_header_generation() - correct field order
  - test_jsonl_valid_json() - each line parses as JSON
  - test_export_empty_result() - no crash on empty data

- E2E test script (tests/e2e/export-formats.sh):
  - Seed SQLite with test data
  - Export to CSV, validate header and row count
  - Export to JSONL, validate each line is valid JSON
  - Test --since/--until filters
  - Test --provider filter
  - Test --domain filter
  - Roundtrip: export CSV, import to new SQLite, compare counts
  - Log: filter inputs, row counts, sample output

- Documentation:
  - README section: 'Exporting Data'
  - Example: 'rano export --format csv --since 24h > daily.csv'
  - Example: 'rano export --format jsonl | jq -r .domain | sort | uniq -c'
  - Field reference table","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:52.683237477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:58.073486759Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-uj6","depends_on_id":"bd-1gl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:58.073466211Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-uj6","depends_on_id":"bd-1vz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T05:01:52.694725221Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,106 @@
+# Preset System Design
+
+Status: design for bead bd-db8 (parent: bd-26d)
+
+## Overview
+
+Presets provide named bundles of configuration values to quickly switch monitoring modes.
+Presets are applied like config files, with deterministic precedence and merge rules.
+
+## File Format and Location
+
+- **Format**: same `key=value` style as `config.conf` (one setting per line, `#` comments)
+- **Location**: `~/.config/rano/presets/<name>.conf`
+- **Encoding**: UTF-8
+
+Example preset file:
+
+```ini
+# ~/.config/rano/presets/audit.conf
+summary_only=true
+stats_interval_ms=0
+include_udp=false
+no_dns=false
+```
+
+## Precedence Rules
+
+Settings apply in this order (later wins):
+
+1. Defaults (hard-coded in binary)
+2. `config.conf`
+3. Preset(s) in the order specified on the CLI
+4. CLI flags
+
+If multiple `--preset` flags are used, they are merged in order:
+
+```bash
+rano --preset quiet --preset audit
+# audit overrides quiet when they set the same key
+```
+
+## Built-in Presets (Embedded)
+
+Built-in presets are compiled into the binary and are always available.
+User presets override built-in presets of the same name.
+
+| Preset | Purpose | Key settings (example) |
+|--------|---------|------------------------|
+| `audit` | Security review / minimal noise | `summary_only=true`, `stats_interval_ms=0`, `include_udp=false`, `no_dns=false`, `log_format=json` |
+| `quiet` | Reduce terminal output | `summary_only=true`, `stats_interval_ms=0`, `no_banner=true` |
+| `live` | Real-time monitoring | `stats_interval_ms=2000`, `stats_view=provider,domain`, `stats_cycle_ms=5000` |
+| `verbose` | Maximum detail | `include_udp=true`, `include_listening=true`, `stats_interval_ms=1000`, `stats_top=10` |
+
+Notes:
+- These are defaults; users can override any value via CLI or user presets.
+- Presets should not set values that require elevated privileges (e.g., forcing pcap).
+
+## CLI Design
+
+### Flags
+
+- `--preset <name>`: Load a named preset (repeatable)
+- `--list-presets`: Print available presets with short descriptions and exit
+
+### Example Usage
+
+```bash
+# Use built-in audit preset
+rano --preset audit
+
+# Merge multiple presets
+rano --preset quiet --preset audit
+
+# Use user-defined preset
+rano --preset team-default
+
+# List all presets
+rano --list-presets
+```
+
+## Error Handling
+
+- **Unknown preset**: print available presets (built-in + user) and exit with error.
+- **Invalid preset file**: warn, skip invalid lines, continue processing remaining lines.
+- **Unreadable preset file**: warn and ignore the preset.
+
+## Output Format for --list-presets
+
+Example output:
+
+```
+Available presets:
+  audit    - Security review / minimal noise
+  quiet    - Reduce terminal output
+  live     - Real-time monitoring focus
+  verbose  - Maximum detail
+  team-default (user) - ~/.config/rano/presets/team-default.conf
+```
+
+## Implementation Notes
+
+- Reuse the existing config parser for preset files to avoid divergent behavior.
+- Merge order: config.conf -> preset(s) -> CLI flags.
+- Built-in presets can be stored as static strings keyed by name.
+- User presets are loaded from `~/.config/rano/presets/` and override built-ins of the same name.
+
